description_long: This bungalow features a prominent gable roof with dark shingles and exposed wooden beams, giving it a classic suburban appearance. The front facade is dominated by large, floor-to-ceiling glass windows and double doors framed in natural wood, allowing ample light into the interior. The lower portion of the exterior walls is finished with red brickwork, providing a sturdy base and visual contrast to the glass and wood above. Both sides of the bungalow have tall, narrow windows arranged in a row, also framed in wood, enhancing the sense of openness and connection to the outdoors. The rear of the bungalow mirrors the front with a similar gable structure but features an open truss and no doors, suggesting a possible patio or open extension. The overall design is modern yet warm, blending traditional materials with contemporary glazing.
description: A modern bungalow with a gable roof, large glass windows, wood framing, and brick base, blending traditional and contemporary elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing large glass double doors, triangular clerestory windows, and brick base beneath the wood-framed facade.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a row of tall, narrow windows and the overhanging gable roof with exposed rafters.
description_view_2: Rear view with an open gable truss, no doors, and a similar brick and wood construction as the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a matching row of tall windows and visible brickwork at the base.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side windows
